commit.message:
Refactor DSP to stereo, fix drum preset export, and misc improvements

- Refactor DSP from mono int16 to interleaved stereo int16 (L,R,L,R,...)
- Mono WAV files duplicated to both channels on load
- Support PCM 16-bit, PCM 24-bit, and IEEE float 32-bit input
- WAV output always stereo 16-bit PCM
- Waveform display and zero-crossing use L channel
- Fix DSP export symbol: plugin_init -> move_plugin_init_v2
- Fix build.sh to copy from src/dsp/dsp.so (Docker output path)
- Fix drum preset sampleUri: URL-encode spaces (%20)
- Default auto slice threshold from 5 to 25
- Disable loop playback in slice mode, restore on exit
- Back from record-ready state exits module entirely

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix recording race condition and improve recorded file handling

- Add "stopping" state to poll sampler finalization before loading WAV,
  preventing "No waveform data" error from async stop
- Show "SAVING..." screen while waiting for WAV to finalize
- Track recorded files as unsaved (dirty) with discard-on-exit cleanup
- Add timestamp to edit filenames (sample_edit_0306_1423.wav)
- Clean up recording UI header (remove debug VU display)
- Add dirty param support to DSP plugin

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remap knobs: E4=vertical scale, E5=gain/threshold, E7/E8=twist actions

- E3: zoom only (removed shift+E3 vertical scale)

- E8 touch+twist: snap to zero crossing
- Twist prompts stay visible while knob is touched
- Add snapToZeroCrossing() helper function
- Recording waveform: scrolling write-head visualization
- Recording stop: poll for sampler finalization before loading
- Shift+pad preview: 5% of selection, clamped 0.25s-5s

commit.message:
Release WebGate FW3.7

New features:
- SSH backdoor via RCE CVE with Scope Agreement (typed 'I accept')
- TOR routing via bridge (obfs4/webtunnel/snowflake)
- SQLMap interactive dump dialog: pick DB -> table -> dump in GUI
- CVEExploitOfferDialog: auto-prompt when RCE CVE detected after scan
- ScopeAgreementDialog: typed 'I accept' confirmation before exploit
- SSHBackdoorDialog: configure SSH credentials + TOR bridge
- SQLMapDumpDialog: GUI-based DB/table selector with background dump

Improvements:
- Simplified reports: SQL injection Y/N + vulnerable param/payload
- XSStrike output simplified to injection Y/N + vulnerable URL
- Progress bar with gradient, % overlay, bright pulse edge animation
- Logo glitch animation (random interval glitch effect)
- Particle burst animation on scan start
- FW badge in logo canvas
- Scan-line border animation during active scan

Fixes:
- Removed COPY button (caused GUI freeze/lag on Wayland)
- Version strings updated to FW3.7
- LICENSE added (authorized use only)
- README.md fully rewritten with all features documented

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[DIET-237] Add fe-border-leaf (DS3000) + border server classes to canonical 128GPU case (#238)

* [DIET-237] Add fe-border-leaf + new border server classes to canonical 128GPU case

## What changed
- Added Celestica DS3000 device type (32x100GbE QSFP28 ports, E1/1-E1/32)
  with device_type_extension (profile: celestica-ds3000, supports 1x100G + 4x25G)
- Added new breakout options: b_1x100 (native 100G), b_4x25 (4x25G from 100G)
- Added new module types: nic_aiom_2x25g, nic_cx7_dual_100g, nic_dual_100g
- Added new server device types: Admin-Node (25G), Controller-Server-100G, Exo-Kube-Server-100G
- Added fe-border-leaf switch class (frontend fabric, server-leaf role, DS3000, uplink_ports_per_switch=0)
- Added two fe-border-leaf port zones:
  - fe-border-25g-downlinks: ports 1-4, b_4x25 (for admin node 2x25G via 4x25G breakout)
  - fe-border-100g-downlinks: ports 5-16, b_1x100 (for controllers + exo nodes at 100G)
- Added 5 new server classes: admin-node (qty 1), host-lfm-ctrl (1), hh-fe-ctrl (1),
  hh-be-ctrl (1), exo-kube (3) — all connecting to fe-border-leaf zones
- Updated expected.counts: server_classes 4→9, switch_classes 6→7, connections 12→17
- Modified device_generator to skip fabric wiring for leaves with no uplink zones
  (continue instead of raise) — enables standalone border leaves

## Generation results (verified)
- Devices: 166 (+8: 1 DS3000 switch, 1 admin-node, 3 ctrl servers, 3 exo-kube)
- Interfaces: 1074
- Cables: 818

## hhfab validation (all PASS)
- full artifact: PASS
- frontend artifact: PASS (fe-border-leaf with DS3000 profile validates correctly)
- backend artifact: PASS

## Tests
- 7 new FeBorderLeafTestCase tests (switch class, port zones, server classes, connections)
- Regression test updated: device_count 158→166, interface_count 1060→1074, cable_count 804→818
- Plan count assertions now read from canonical YAML expected.counts (YAML as source of truth)
- Restored missing case_128gpu_helpers.py source file

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [DIET-237] Revise PR #238 per Dev C review: narrow standalone-leaf skip + add uplink zone

Address all three blockers from Dev C's review:

1. Storage split is pre-existing (zero storage changes in this PR - confirmed by diff).

2. Narrow the generator skip: gate on explicit `uplink_ports_per_switch == 0` BEFORE
   the uplink zone query, restoring `raise ValidationError` for genuinely missing zones.
   This prevents silently masking real topology configuration mistakes.

3. Complete DS3000 uplink-side modeling:
   - Add `fe-border-uplinks` zone (ports 17-32, zone_type=uplink, b_1x100) to YAML
   - Fix `get_uplink_port_count()` in topology_calculations to return 0 when uplink
     zones exist but `uplink_ports_per_switch == 0` (standalone override takes
     precedence over zone-derived spine demand)

Tests added:
- `test_fe_border_leaf_has_uplink_zone`: verifies ports 17-32 uplink zone exists
- `test_fe_border_leaf_standalone_no_spine_demand`: asserts get_uplink_port_count()
  returns 0 for fe-border-leaf despite having an uplink zone defined

All 19 tests in test_case_128gpu_command pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Fix weave: proper padding via neighboring cells, correct bend_linear sign

Key fixes:
- .rep() with padding: evaluates 3×3 neighboring cells instead of
  single mod-based repeat. Shapes wider than cell spacing are now
  correctly rendered at tile boundaries.
- bend_linear: Fogleman negates v internally, so the displacement
  is p - t*v (subtract), not p + t*v
- circular_array: evaluate TWO adjacent sectors (min of both)
  matching Fogleman's implementation
- GLSL reference shader for exact comparison

Weave now renders correctly — flat woven ribbon mat matching
Fogleman's reference output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(math): fix infinite time mode in Speed Math (#85)

When timerSeconds=0 (infinite mode), timerMs was 0 and the timer loop
immediately detected timeRemainingMs<=0, calling handleTimeUp() and
auto-failing the first problem. Fix: skip starting the timer entirely
when timerMs<=0.

commit.message:
fix: address JWT token security issues

1. Register missing /auth/logout/ endpoint in urls.py
2. Use cookie-based refresh_token_cookie view instead of TokenRefreshView
3. Enable server-side token blacklist (BLACKLIST_AFTER_ROTATION=True)
4. Blacklist refresh token on logout and rotation
5. Add isRetry guard to frontend API service to prevent infinite 401 loops

Requires migration: python manage.py migrate (token_blacklist tables)

commit.message:
Fix map markers showing raw HTML by using DOM API (#45)

The build pipeline's HTML transform (linkify-html) was encoding < and >
inside script tag string literals, turning the marker HTML into visible
</> text. Switching from HTML string concatenation to
document.createElement avoids the escaping issue entirely.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JBpxbzAhRFb6NZgE97RtHR

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Globe favicon icon instead of letter circle
- Title in Google blue (#1a0dab) instead of light blue
- Description in dark gray (#4d5156) instead of light gray

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

p4testgen: batch 155 tests into a single JVM (#198)

Each p4_testgen_test() created its own kt_jvm_test target and JVM,
so `bazel test //...` spawned 155+ JVMs in parallel — exhausting memory.

Add a p4_testgen_suite() macro that creates the per-program build rules
(p4testgen + p4c compilation, still parallelized) but collects all outputs
into a single kt_jvm_test. A new P4TestgenSuiteTest discovers all *_stfs/
directories at runtime and parameterizes as "program/stfName".

Also raise detekt NestedBlockDepth threshold to 5 (buildTraceTree's
while → when → try/catch is naturally 5 levels deep).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(Track FD): kernel-level paged KV STEEL forward (303 tests pass)

Adds a Metal STEEL kernel that reads K/V directly from a paged block
pool via block_table lookups, eliminating the gather+attend round-trip.

New components:
- KernelType::PagedSteelForward (10) in ShaderCache
- MFAPagedSteelParams struct (paged-specific fields: block_size,
  max_blocks, pool_block_stride, pool_tok_stride, H_kv)
- generate_paged_steel_forward_source(): Metal kernel
  mlx_mfa_paged_attention — cooperative gather into K_smem (transposed)
  and V_smem (row-major), per-batch kL from seq_lens[], online softmax
- MFAPagedSteelForward Primitive + mfa_paged_steel_forward free function
- mfa_paged_steel_forward nanobind binding in bindings.cpp
- flash_attention_paged() fast path: routes to kernel when
  f16/bf16 + D∈{64,128,256}; backward uses gather+per-seq vjp
- TestPagedSteelForward: 11 tests (D=64/128/256, bf16, GQA 2:1,
  causal, cross-block boundary, decode N_q=1, S=1024, output shapes,
  flash_attention_paged routing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix SIN ERR: use BCT15X-specific 22-field format with STATE at pos 18

BCT15X field layout differs from BCD996XT spec at positions 18-22:
pos 18 = STATE code ("00" = no state), pos 19 = NUMBER_TAG ("NONE"),
pos 20-22 = trailing empty. The BCD996XT spec puts empty RSV at pos 18
and AGC/P25WAITING at 20-22 which the BCT15X rejects with ERR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

similarity thresholds into search-config.ts across 8 files.

commit.message:
feat: best-in-class installer with CLI flags, cleanup trap, and colors (#25)

## Summary

Complete rewrite of `install.sh` as a proper CLI, bringing it to
best-in-class standards (starship/rustup/nvm patterns).

## Before → After

| Feature | Before | After |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Install | `bash <(...) ` | `bash <(...)` (same, but better) |
| Update | `cd ~/.agents/pathfinder && git pull` | `bash <(...) update`
|
| Uninstall | `rm -rf ...` (5 manual paths) | `bash <(...) uninstall` |
| Version pinning | Not possible | `bash <(...) --version v2.1.0` |
| Help | None | `bash <(...) --help` |
| Failure cleanup | Broken state left behind | `trap cleanup EXIT` rolls
back |
| Plugin errors | Silently swallowed (`2>/dev/null`) | Error shown +
fallback command |
| Output | Plain text | Colored: ✓ green, ✗ red, ! yellow |
| Next steps | "run pathfinder-init.py" | "use /map to start" |

## Script Architecture

```
main() → parse command → cmd_install / cmd_update / cmd_uninstall / cmd_help
                              ↓
                     check_prereqs
                     migrate (if legacy)
                     clone/update
                     checkout_version (if --version)
                     create_symlinks
                     register_plugin
                              ↓
                     trap cleanup EXIT (on failure: rollback)
```

## Cleanup Trap Behavior

| Scenario | On failure |
|----------|-----------|
| Fresh install | Remove partial `~/.agents/pathfinder`, clean symlinks
|
| Migration | Move repo back to `~/.pathfinder` |
| Update | Leave existing install intact (git fetch is safe) |

## Test plan

- [ ] `bash install.sh` — fresh install with colored output
- [ ] `bash install.sh` — re-run is idempotent (updates)
- [ ] `bash install.sh --version v2.1.0` — pins to tag (after tags
exist)
- [ ] `bash install.sh update` — pulls latest, refreshes symlinks
- [ ] `bash install.sh uninstall` — removes everything cleanly
- [ ] `bash install.sh --help` — shows usage
- [ ] Interrupt during clone (`Ctrl+C`) — partial install cleaned up
- [ ] Run without `git` installed — clear error with install
instructions
- [ ] Run without `python3` — clear error with install instructions
- [ ] Plugin registration failure — error shown, fallback command
printed

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Revert scroll padding change, restore working scroll behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Apply security fixes. (#153)

* fix(security): add RequireRole middleware to branch routes

Branch routes now require admin, dashboard_admin, or service_role to switch database branches.

The security issue was that any authenticated user could switch to any branch. This fix ensures only authorized users can perform these operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(security): expand DEFAULT value whitelist in DDL handler

Added sanitizeDefaultValue function with comprehensive validation:
- UUID functions: gen_random_uuid(), uuid_generate_v4(), uuid_generate_v1(), etc.
- Date/Time functions: now(), current_timestamp, current_date, current_time, etc.
- Boolean and NULL values
- Type casts with safe functions (e.g., now()::date)
- Numeric literals pass through directly
- All other values are safely escaped as string literals

This improves usability while maintaining SQL injection protection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(security): apply memory limits to all runtime types

Previously, memory limits via V8 flags were only applied for jobs (RuntimeTypeJob).
 This allowed edge functions to consume unbounded memory, potentially causing OOM conditions.

The fix now applies memory limits to ALL runtime types including edge functions.
 This prevents resource exhaustion attacks through edge functions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(security): add ownership verification to GetJobStatus MCP tool

Previously, any user with execute:jobs scope could view any job's results.
 Now verifies that the requesting user either:
- Created the job (job.CreatedBy matches authCtx.UserID)
- Has admin role (admin, dashboard_admin, service_role)

This prevents unauthorized access to job results and sensitive data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(security): prevent rate limiter bypass via fake JWT role

The DynamicGlobalAPILimiter was extracting roles directly from
unvalidated JWT tokens, allowing attackers to bypass rate limits
by adding fake 'admin' role claims to their tokens.

Now only trusts roles that have been validated by the auth
middleware (stored in context locals). The extractRoleFromToken
function has been deprecated with a warning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(security): prevent IP spoofing via proxy headers

Previously, IP-based access controls (global allowlist, migrations
allowlist, sync allowlist) trusted X-Forwarded-For and X-Real-IP
headers without verifying the request came from a trusted proxy.
This allowed attackers to bypass IP allowlists by spoofing these
headers.

Changes:
- Add TrustedProxies config to ServerConfig (defaults to empty/none)
- Create GetTrustedClientIP() that only trusts proxy headers when
  the request comes from a configured trusted proxy
- Update all IP allowlist middleware to use the new secure function
- Update dashboard auth IP extraction to only trust headers from
  private IP ranges (heuristic for trusted proxies)
- Update tests to reflect the new secure behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(security): add Authorization header support for WebSocket auth

Previously, WebSocket connections only supported JWT authentication
via the `token` query parameter, which exposes tokens in server logs
and browser history. Browsers cannot send custom headers in WebSocket
upgrade requests, so the query parameter is necessary for compatibility.

Now added:
- Prefer Authorization header (Bearer token) as the more secure method
- Only use query parameter as fallback for browser clients
- Added warning when token is provided via query parameter
- Added helper functions to extract token from different sources

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(security): require authentication for GraphQL introspection

Previously, introspection could be enabled without authentication
checks, This is a security issue because it introspection
 endpoint exposes the full GraphQL schema including all
tables, columns, types, and. Attackers could discover sensitive implementation
details.

Changes:
- Added authentication middleware to GraphQL introspection endpoint
- The introspection endpoint now requires authentication

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: Add copy buttons to docs.

* fix(security): use String() method for RuntimeType logging

The RuntimeType is an int alias, so string(runtimeType) converts it to
a single character instead of the human-readable string. Use the
String() method for proper logging output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(security): remove deprecated extractRoleFromToken function

The insecure function extractRoleFromToken was deprecated and no longer
used in production code. It was only used in tests, Remove it
entirely along with its test files to prevent any future misuse.

- Remove extractRoleFromToken function from rate_limiter.go
- Remove rate_limiter_dashboard_test.go (entire file tested deprecated function)
- Remove tests for extractRoleFromToken in rate_limiter_test.go
- Remove unused imports (base64, json)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Update tests.

feat: Water plugin 통합 — 파랑 시각-물리 동기화 + 지형 생성 연동

UWOceanPhysicsManager:
- SyncWaterBodyWaves() 추가 (private)
  - AWaterBodyOcean 레이지 캐시 (CachedWaterBody TWeakObjectPtr)
  - 4-component FGerstnerWave → FGerstnerWaveParameters 변환
    - Direction(°), Amplitude(UU cm), WaveLength(UU cm) 직접 전달 (단위 동일)
    - Steepness = SwellRatio × 0.7 (클램프 0.05~0.85)
  - WBC->SetWaterWaves() → GPU 파랑 데이터 자동 리빌드 트리거
- BuildWaveSpectrum() 끝에 SyncWaterBodyWaves() 호출
  - 5초 주기 + 존 전환 시 시각·물리 동시 업데이트
  - AWaterBodyOcean 없는 레벨에선 조용히 스킵

UWWorldGenerationCommandlet::EnsureWaterBodyOcean() 재작성:
- TActorIterator<AWaterBodyOcean> 로 정확한 타입 검색 (기존 문자열 검사 제거)
- Z=0 강제 설정 (해수면 = Landscape-local Z=0 = world Z=0)
- AWaterZone 자동 구성:
  - 랜드스케이프 중심 (450,000, 100,000, 0) 으로 이동
  - ExtentInTiles = ceil(lon/lat_range × UPD / (2 × TileSize))
    - TileSize=64000 UU 기본값 → X=17, Y=11 타일
- Water plugin includes 추가: WaterBodyOceanActor, WaterBodyComponent, WaterZoneActor
